questions.question: Why don't scoped styles apply to content inside <Teleport>?
options: Teleport creates a new Vue application instance that ignores parent styles
Teleported elements move to a different DOM location, leaving the component's subtree where scoped attributes are matched
Scoped CSS is disabled when Teleport is detected in the same component
The browser blocks style inheritance across different DOM subtrees

questions.question: What is the recommended approach for styling teleported content without polluting global styles?
options: Use inline styles on all teleported elements
Use CSS Modules — class names are hashed at build time, so scoping doesn't depend on DOM position
Add the data-v attribute manually to teleported elements

questions.question: Which option works but is considered a poor practice for styling teleported content?
options: Using a separate non-scoped <style> block with prefixed class names
Using :deep() in a scoped block — it works but defeats the purpose of scoping
Using CSS Modules for the teleported elements
Moving teleported content into its own component with its own styles
